summary: Clear audience recording with a few track‑label glitches, highlighted by a stellar Peggy‑O and a scorching Big Railroad Blues.
review: The tape captures an engaging fall‑’83 show – the band rolls through a rare first‑set Samson & Delilah, delivers a smoking Eyes > Women transition, and treats listeners to a standout Peggy‑O and one of the tour’s best Big Railroad Blues.  Sound quality is solid and listenable for an audience source, though combined tracks throw off later labeling.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the performance (Peggy‑O, Big Railroad, Eyes > Women) and find the audience recording listenable; only one review is strongly negative, leading to an overall mixed‑positive assessment.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear and listenable overall, but several tracks are combined or mislabeled (Shakedown/Samson, Eyes/Women) causing confusion in the track list.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Rare first‑set Samson & Delilah and a hurried finish with a very short post‑drums section; overall flow energetic but the ending feels rushed.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for heavy guitar work on Big Railroad Blues, playing both songs in the Eyes > Women transition, and generally dominating the fall ’83 tour.
band_member_comments.Bob: Dedicates a botched Baby Blue to the Dodgers; no specific performance comments.
band_member_comments.Drums: Post‑drums section noted as unusually short, suggesting the band was in a hurry.
